facts.text: Victory Gardens earned its name from the victory gardens planted at private homes during World War II.
In 1941 the federal government bought 91 acres in Randolph Township to build a 300‑unit housing project for war‑industry workers.
The borough was officially incorporated on June 20, 1951 after a September 1951 referendum that moved 1,300 residents into an independent municipality.
A 1973 project added 184 units of garden apartments on 12.4 acres, replacing temporary wartime housing with permanent homes for about 400 people.
Victory Gardens sits within Morris County, one of New Jersey’s 564 municipalities, where the borough form is the most common local government type.
When the federal government built the original housing, it covered every infrastructure cost, leaving the community with fully equipped homes from day one.
